analysis: Seven nights running without dropping below 6h 54m — that's not an accident, that's a whole routine holding you together. Your REM is doing something interesting: it peaked at 2h 34m on the second night and stayed mostly strong through the stretch, which means your brain is processing, consolidating, doing its little overnight filing. Deep sleep flipped the other way — quiet early on, then your two best deep nights landed back-to-back in the middle of the week at 53 and 49 minutes, which is your body deciding to get serious. The one thing worth noticing is that awake time crept up toward the end, hitting over an hour on the 7th, before last night pulled it back to 32 minutes and gave you a clean 7h 56m to close on. Whatever you did last night, you did it right.
